entries.purpose: Per-slice receive controls: mode, frequency tuning, RX/TX antenna selection, filter width, AGC, AF gain/pan, squelch, RIT/XIT, and FM repeater duplex settings.
entries.how_to_open: Always visible in Applet Panel (right sidebar). Toggle with the 'RX' tray button on the right sidebar.

controls.label: Slice tabs (A..H)
controls.behavior: Selects which slice the RX applet is bound to; emits sliceActivationRequested.
controls.notes: Row hidden if maxSlices <= 1.

controls.label: Slice badge
controls.behavior: Displays the letter of the currently bound slice.
controls.notes: Coloured by slice identity.

controls.label: 🔓 / 🔒
controls.behavior: Toggles tune-lock on the slice; locked slice ignores frequency changes.
controls.notes: Icon flips between open and closed padlock.

controls.label: ANT1 (RX antenna)
controls.behavior: Opens a menu listing available antennas; selecting sets slice->setRxAntenna.
controls.notes: Populated from the radio's ant_list; blue-coloured label.

controls.label: ANT1 (TX antenna)
controls.behavior: Opens a menu listing TX-capable antennas; sets slice->setTxAntenna.
controls.notes: Red-coloured label; RX-only antenna ports (prefix 'RX') are filtered out.

controls.label: 2.7K (filter width)
controls.behavior: Shows current filter width in kHz.
controls.notes: Updates when filter preset is applied.

controls.label: QSK
controls.behavior: Lights amber when CW break-in (QSK) is active.
controls.notes: Read-only; controlled via the CW applet Breakin button.

controls.label: TX (badge)
controls.behavior: Click to set this slice as the TX slice (calls slice->setTxSlice).

controls.label: Mode combo
controls.valid_range: USB, LSB, CW, AM, SAM, FM, NFM, DFM, DIGU, DIGL, RTTY (+ RADE if HAVE_RADE)
controls.behavior: Sets slice mode; reshapes filter and step presets for the new mode.
controls.notes: RADE option requires HAVE_RADE build flag.

controls.label: Frequency label
controls.behavior: Displays current VFO frequency with dotted grouping.
controls.notes: Click to switch into edit mode.

controls.label: Frequency edit
controls.behavior: Enter MHz and press Enter to tune and recenter; supports kHz/Hz auto-scaling.
controls.notes: XVTR-aware: accepts up to 450 MHz when slice is on an XVTR antenna.

controls.label: STEP
controls.behavior: < / > or mousewheel cycles through per-mode step sizes; emits stepSizeChanged.
controls.notes: Step list depends on slice mode.

controls.label: Filter width presets
controls.behavior: Click to apply a preset filter width; right-click to save current width as a preset.
controls.notes: Buttons hidden for FM/NFM/DFM modes; presets are per-mode.

controls.label: Filter passband widget
controls.behavior: Drag the lo/hi edges to adjust filter passband; emits filterChanged (lo, hi).

controls.label: Tone mode (FM)
controls.behavior: Selects CTCSS tone mode on FM/NFM/DFM.
controls.notes: Visible only in FM family modes.

controls.label: CTCSS tone value
controls.valid_range: 41 standard EIA/TIA-603 tones (67.0 Hz to 254.1 Hz)
controls.behavior: Selects CTCSS tone frequency sent with transmit.
controls.notes: Enabled only when Tone mode = CTCSS TX.

controls.label: Offset (FM)
controls.behavior: Sets FM repeater offset frequency in MHz.

controls.label: − (offset down)
controls.behavior: Sets repeater offset direction to 'down' (TX below RX).

controls.label: Simplex
controls.behavior: Sets repeater offset direction to simplex (TX = RX).

controls.label: + (offset up)
controls.behavior: Sets repeater offset direction to 'up' (TX above RX).

controls.label: REV
controls.behavior: Inverts the TX offset sign to work a reversed repeater pair.

controls.label: 🔊 / 🔇 (mute)
controls.behavior: Mutes the slice audio output.

controls.label: AF gain
controls.behavior: Adjusts slice audio output gain; emits afGainChanged.

controls.label: L / R pan
controls.behavior: Pans slice audio between left (0) and right (100) channels.
controls.notes: Double-click resets to 50 (centre).

controls.label: SQL
controls.behavior: Enables the squelch at the current slider level.

controls.label: Squelch level
controls.behavior: Adjusts squelch threshold; takes effect only when SQL is on.

controls.label: AGC mode
controls.behavior: Sets the slice AGC mode.
controls.notes: Hidden in FM family modes.

controls.label: AGC threshold
controls.behavior: Sets AGC threshold (or AGC off-level when AGC mode is Off).
controls.notes: Tooltip reflects which value is being adjusted.

controls.label: RIT
controls.behavior: Toggles Receive Incremental Tuning on/off.

controls.label: RIT 0
controls.behavior: Zeroes the RIT offset.

controls.label: RIT offset
controls.behavior: < / > or mousewheel adjusts RIT offset by 10 Hz steps.

controls.label: XIT
controls.behavior: Toggles Transmit Incremental Tuning on/off.

controls.label: XIT 0
controls.behavior: Zeroes the XIT offset.

controls.label: XIT offset
controls.behavior: < / > or mousewheel adjusts XIT offset by 10 Hz steps.

indicators.label: Filter width label
indicators.meaning: Current slice filter bandwidth.

indicators.label: QSK
indicators.meaning: CW full break-in state reflected from the CW applet.

related_user_tasks: Tune the radio to a frequency (type MHz in the readout)
Change mode (USB, LSB, CW, AM, FM, etc.)
Pick a filter width preset for the current mode
Select the RX or TX antenna for this slice
Turn on the squelch and set its threshold
Use RIT to offset the receive frequency for a drifting station
Use XIT to offset the transmit frequency without changing RX
Work an FM repeater with CTCSS tone and +/- offset
Lock the slice to prevent accidental retuning
Switch between multiple slices using the A..H tab row

entries.purpose: Transmit controls: forward power and SWR meters, RF/Tune power sliders, TX profile selector, TUNE/MOX/ATU/MEM buttons, and APD (Adaptive Pre-Distortion) toggle with Active/Cal/Avail status.
entries.how_to_open: Always visible in Applet Panel (right sidebar). Toggle with the 'TX' tray button on the right sidebar.

controls.label: RF Pwr
controls.behavior: Displays forward power at the exciter output.
controls.notes: Scale changes based on radio model via setPowerScale.

controls.label: SWR
controls.behavior: Displays standing wave ratio at the exciter.

controls.label: RF Power
controls.behavior: Sets transmit RF power level (W); calls TransmitModel::setRfPower.

controls.label: Tune Pwr
controls.behavior: Sets tune-carrier power level; calls TransmitModel::setTunePower.

controls.label: TX Profile
controls.behavior: Selecting a profile calls TransmitModel::loadProfile.

controls.label: Success
controls.behavior: Lights green when ATU status is Successful or OK.

controls.label: Byp
controls.behavior: Lights orange when ATU is in Bypass or ManualBypass.

controls.label: Mem
controls.behavior: Lights green when ATU is using a memory.

controls.label: TUNE
controls.behavior: Starts/stops tune carrier; text becomes 'TUNING...' with red background while active.

controls.label: MOX
controls.behavior: Toggles manual transmit; button goes red while TX is keyed.

controls.label: ATU
controls.behavior: Starts the internal ATU tuning cycle.
controls.notes: Disabled when TGXL is in OPERATE mode.

controls.label: MEM
controls.behavior: Toggles ATU memory recall on/off.
controls.notes: Disabled when TGXL is in OPERATE mode.

controls.label: APD
controls.behavior: Toggles adaptive pre-distortion on the radio.

controls.label: Active
controls.behavior: Lit when APD is on and the equalizer is actively applied.

controls.label: Cal
controls.behavior: Lit when APD is on and still calibrating.

controls.label: Avail
controls.behavior: Lit when APD is on and a calibration is available but not yet applied.

indicators.label: TUNE button
indicators.meaning: Idle vs. tune-in-progress.

indicators.label: MOX button
indicators.meaning: Receive vs. keyed transmit.

indicators.label: APD status cluster
indicators.meaning: APD progression: Cal (calibrating) → Avail (ready) → Active (applied).

related_user_tasks: Set RF output power
Set tune-carrier power
Switch TX profiles (e.g. SSB, Digital)
Start a tune carrier to check SWR
Toggle MOX to manually key the transmitter
Run the internal ATU
Recall an ATU memory
Enable APD to linearise the transmitter

entries.purpose: Phone voice TX controls: AM carrier level, VOX enable/level/delay, DEXP noise gate, and TX filter low-cut/high-cut setters.
entries.how_to_open: Always visible in Applet Panel (right sidebar). Toggle with the 'PHNE' tray button on the right sidebar.

controls.label: AM Carrier
controls.behavior: Sets AM carrier power level; calls TransmitModel::setAmCarrierLevel.
controls.notes: Shown with numeric label (e.g. '48').

controls.label: VOX
controls.behavior: Toggles voice-operated transmit; calls TransmitModel::setVoxEnable.

controls.label: VOX level
controls.behavior: Sets VOX activation threshold; calls TransmitModel::setVoxLevel.

controls.label: Delay
controls.behavior: Sets VOX hang time before returning to receive; calls TransmitModel::setVoxDelay.

controls.label: DEXP
controls.behavior: Toggles downward expander (noise gate).
controls.notes: Non-functional on fw v1.4.0.0 — radio returns error 0x5000002D.

controls.label: DEXP threshold
controls.behavior: Sets DEXP gate threshold; persists to DexpLevel.
controls.notes: Same firmware limitation as DEXP toggle.

controls.label: Low Cut < / >
controls.behavior: < / > or mousewheel adjusts TX filter low-cut by 50 Hz.

controls.label: High Cut < / >
controls.behavior: < / > or mousewheel adjusts TX filter high-cut by 50 Hz.

related_user_tasks: Adjust AM carrier power for AM transmit
Enable VOX and set trigger threshold
Tune VOX hang time
Set the TX audio low-cut frequency
Set the TX audio high-cut frequency

entries.purpose: Mode-aware transmit panel: shows Phone (mic/processor/monitor) controls in voice modes, automatically switches to CW controls (delay, speed, sidetone, iambic, pitch) when the active slice is in CW mode.
entries.how_to_open: Always visible in Applet Panel (right sidebar). Toggle with the 'P/CW' tray button on the right sidebar.

controls.label: Level
controls.behavior: Shows microphone input peak level in dBFS (Phone panel).
controls.notes: Suppressed to -150 when met_in_rx is off and not transmitting.

controls.label: Compression
controls.behavior: Shows speech compression amount in dB (Phone panel).

controls.label: Mic profile
controls.behavior: Loads the named mic processing profile; calls TransmitModel::loadMicProfile.

controls.label: Mic source
controls.valid_range: MIC, BAL, LINE, ACC, PC (plus any from micInputList())
controls.behavior: Selects microphone input source; calls TransmitModel::setMicSelection.

controls.label: Mic gain
controls.behavior: Adjusts mic input level; for 'PC' source uses local PcMicGain persistence.
controls.notes: Radio always reports mic_level=0 when source=PC; value kept client-side.

controls.label: +ACC
controls.behavior: Enables the accessory mic input mix; calls TransmitModel::setMicAcc.

controls.label: PROC
controls.behavior: Toggles the speech processor; calls TransmitModel::setSpeechProcessorEnable.

controls.label: NOR/DX/DX+
controls.behavior: Three-position processor level; calls TransmitModel::setSpeechProcessorLevel.

controls.label: DAX
controls.behavior: Enables DAX as the TX audio source; calls TransmitModel::setDax.

controls.label: MON
controls.behavior: Enables TX sidetone monitor; calls TransmitModel::setSbMonitor.

controls.label: Monitor volume
controls.behavior: Sets sideband monitor volume; calls TransmitModel::setMonGainSb.

controls.label: ALC
controls.behavior: Shows automatic level control reading on the CW panel.

controls.label: Delay (CW)
controls.behavior: Sets CW break-in delay; calls TransmitModel::setCwDelay.

controls.label: Speed (CW)
controls.behavior: Sets CW keying speed; calls TransmitModel::setCwSpeed.

controls.label: Sidetone
controls.behavior: Toggles CW sidetone monitor; calls TransmitModel::setCwSidetone.

controls.label: Sidetone volume
controls.behavior: Sets CW monitor volume; calls TransmitModel::setMonGainCw.

controls.label: L / R pan (CW)
controls.behavior: CW monitor pan slider (wiring pending a mon_pan_cw model field).

controls.label: Breakin
controls.behavior: Toggles full break-in (QSK); calls TransmitModel::setCwBreakIn.

controls.label: Iambic
controls.behavior: Toggles iambic paddle keyer; calls TransmitModel::setCwIambic.

controls.label: Pitch < / >
controls.behavior: Steps the CW sidetone/decode pitch by 10 Hz; calls TransmitModel::setCwPitch.

indicators.label: Level gauge
indicators.meaning: Microphone peak level.

indicators.label: Compression gauge
indicators.meaning: Speech compression amount (reversed fill).

indicators.label: ALC gauge
indicators.meaning: Automatic level control (CW sub-panel).

related_user_tasks: Pick a mic source (MIC, BAL, LINE, ACC, PC)
Adjust mic gain and enable the accessory mix
Enable speech processor at NOR, DX, or DX+ level
Listen to a TX sidetone monitor
Select a mic profile for a specific microphone
Set CW keying speed in WPM
Set CW break-in delay
Enable iambic paddle keying
Change CW pitch / sidetone frequency

entries.purpose: Runs up to four rigctld-compatible TCP servers (and PTY symlinks on Linux/macOS) so external logging and contest software can control one slice per channel.
entries.how_to_open: Hidden by default. Toggle with the 'CAT' tray button on the right sidebar.

controls.label: Enable TCP
controls.behavior: Starts/stops all four rigctld TCP servers on Base..Base+3.
controls.notes: Also persists the current base port to CatTcpPort.

controls.label: Enable TTY
controls.behavior: Starts/stops all four PTY symlinks under /tmp/AetherSDR-CAT-A..D.

controls.label: Base
controls.behavior: Base TCP port; channels bind to port, port+1, port+2, port+3.
controls.notes: Out-of-range values snap back to 4532; servers restart with new port if currently enabled.

controls.label: A/B/C/D channel rows
controls.behavior: Each row shows the channel badge, TCP status, and PTY path.
controls.notes: Slice-colour coded badges.

indicators.label: Per-channel TCP status
indicators.meaning: Server state and connected client count per channel.

indicators.label: Per-channel PTY path
indicators.meaning: Symlink path logging software can open as a serial device.

related_user_tasks: Enable CAT TCP so N1MM, Log4OM, WSJT-X can control the radio
Enable CAT PTY so Linux/macOS apps can open a serial-style CAT port
Change the base TCP port
Autostart CAT servers with AetherSDR
Check how many external clients are connected to each channel

entries.purpose: DAX audio bridge: shows per-channel RX meters and gain sliders for DAX 1-4 plus a single TX meter, with a master Enable toggle that persists as AutoStartDAX.
entries.how_to_open: Hidden by default. Toggle with the 'DAX' tray button on the right sidebar.

controls.label: DAX Enable
controls.behavior: Starts the DAX audio bridge; emits daxToggled.
controls.notes: Button label is 'Enable'; master switch for all DAX RX and TX streams.

controls.label: DAX 1 gain+meter
controls.behavior: Combined meter/slider; drag to set RX gain on DAX channel 1.
controls.notes: Emits daxRxGainChanged(1, g) and persists.

controls.label: DAX 2 gain+meter
controls.behavior: Combined meter/slider; drag to set RX gain on DAX channel 2.

controls.label: DAX 3 gain+meter
controls.behavior: Combined meter/slider; drag to set RX gain on DAX channel 3.

controls.label: DAX 4 gain+meter
controls.behavior: Combined meter/slider; drag to set RX gain on DAX channel 4.

controls.label: TX gain+meter
controls.behavior: Combined meter/slider for the DAX TX stream.

controls.label: Slice-assignment status (per channel)
controls.behavior: Shows which slice is currently routed to each DAX channel.

indicators.label: DAX 1..4 assignment
indicators.meaning: The slice (if any) currently assigned to this DAX channel.

indicators.label: TX assignment
indicators.meaning: The slice currently holding TX privileges (drives DAX TX).

related_user_tasks: Enable DAX to route slice audio to WSJT-X / FLDigi / other digital software
Set DAX RX gain per channel
Set DAX TX gain
Autostart DAX on launch
See which slice is currently using each DAX channel
Identify which slice is the TX slice

entries.purpose: Per-channel DAX IQ stream controls: enable/disable each of four IQ streams, pick a sample rate, and watch a real-time level meter.
entries.how_to_open: Hidden by default. Toggle with the 'IQ' tray button on the right sidebar.

controls.label: IQ 1..4 rate
controls.behavior: Changing the rate emits iqRateChanged(channel, rate).
controls.notes: Combo syncs back to the radio-reported rate when a stream is active.

controls.label: IQ 1..4 meter
controls.behavior: Shows the RMS level of the IQ stream.
controls.notes: Resets to 0 on disconnect or disable.

controls.label: IQ 1..4 Off/On
controls.behavior: Clicking toggles the IQ stream; emits iqEnableRequested or iqDisableRequested.
controls.notes: Button text/style switches between 'Off' and 'On'. Streams are per-session, not persisted by the radio.

indicators.label: IQ channel state
indicators.meaning: Whether this IQ stream is currently active on the radio.

related_user_tasks: Enable an IQ stream for external SDR software
Pick the IQ sample rate (24k/48k/96k/192k)
Monitor the RMS level of each IQ stream
Disable an IQ stream to free bandwidth

entries.purpose: Radio hardware telemetry: PA temperature, supply voltage, and main fan speed gauges.
entries.how_to_open: Hidden by default. Toggle with the 'MTR' tray button on the right sidebar.

controls.label: PA Temp
controls.behavior: Displays PATEMP meter reading from the radio.

controls.label: +13.8V
controls.behavior: Displays +13.8A supply voltage meter.

controls.label: Main Fan
controls.behavior: Displays MAINFAN meter value (resolved lazily by MeterModel::findMeter).
controls.notes: PACURRENT intentionally omitted: the 10A meter range clips under full PA draw on FLEX-8000 hardware.

indicators.label: Radio Hardware header
indicators.meaning: Section title above the gauges.

related_user_tasks: Watch PA temperature during long overs
Check the radio's DC supply voltage
Monitor the main cooling fan speed

entries.purpose: Controls for the 4O3A Tuner Genius XL external antenna tuner: forward power and SWR gauges, C1/L/C2 relay bars, TUNE and OPERATE/BYPASS/STANDBY buttons, and a 3x1 antenna switch when a direct TGXL connection is active.
entries.how_to_open: Hidden until a Tuner Genius XL is detected. When present, toggle with the 'TUN' tray button on the right sidebar.

controls.label: Fwd Pwr
controls.valid_range: 0-200 W barefoot, 0-600 W Aurora, 0-2000 W with PGXL
controls.behavior: Displays TGXL-reported forward power.
controls.notes: Scale set by setPowerScale based on radio/amp.

controls.label: SWR
controls.behavior: Displays TGXL-reported SWR.

controls.label: C1
controls.behavior: Shows the C1 relay bank position; mousewheel scroll calls TunerModel::adjustRelay(0, dir).
controls.notes: Scroll enabled only when direct TGXL connection is active.

controls.label: L
controls.behavior: Shows the L relay bank position; mousewheel scroll calls TunerModel::adjustRelay(1, dir).

controls.label: C2
controls.behavior: Shows the C2 relay bank position; mousewheel scroll calls TunerModel::adjustRelay(2, dir).

controls.label: TUNE
controls.behavior: Calls TunerModel::autoTune; button turns red and reads 'TUNING...' then flashes 'SWR x.xx' when complete.

controls.label: OPERATE
controls.behavior: Cycles OPERATE → BYPASS → STANDBY → OPERATE.
controls.notes: Label and colour change per state (green/orange/default).

controls.label: ANT 1
controls.behavior: Selects antenna port 1 on the TGXL 3x1 switch; calls TunerModel::setAntennaA(1).
controls.notes: Row hidden unless direct TGXL connection is active and antenna switch is present.

controls.label: ANT 2
controls.behavior: Selects antenna port 2; calls TunerModel::setAntennaA(2).

controls.label: ANT 3
controls.behavior: Selects antenna port 3; calls TunerModel::setAntennaA(3).

indicators.label: TUNE button
indicators.meaning: Idle / tuning in progress / captured post-tune SWR (flashes for 2.5 s).

indicators.label: OPERATE button
indicators.meaning: TGXL relay/bypass state.

related_user_tasks: Run an autotune on the external TGXL
Put the tuner in OPERATE, BYPASS, or STANDBY
Fine-tune the C1/L/C2 relays with the mousewheel
Switch between three antennas on a TGXL 3x1
Read SWR immediately after a tune

entries.purpose: Telemetry and OPERATE/STANDBY control for an external Power Genius XL amplifier: forward power, SWR, temperature, drain current, mains voltage, and mode.
entries.how_to_open: Hidden until a Power Genius XL amplifier is detected. When present, toggle with the 'AMP' tray button on the right sidebar.

controls.label: Fwd Pwr
controls.behavior: Shows PGXL forward power.

controls.label: SWR
controls.behavior: Shows PGXL SWR.

controls.label: Temp
controls.behavior: Shows PGXL heatsink temperature.

controls.label: Volts / Amps
controls.behavior: Text label 'Volts: xxxV  Amps: x.xA' updated from setMainsVoltage and setDrainCurrent.
controls.notes: Hidden until first telemetry arrives.

controls.label: MEffA
controls.behavior: Displays the PGXL MEffA value.
controls.notes: Hidden until setMeff is called.

controls.label: OPERATE
controls.behavior: Toggles the amplifier between OPERATE and STANDBY; emits operateToggled.
controls.notes: Hidden until setState arrives. Shows 'OPERATE' (green) for IDLE/OPERATE/TRANSMIT_* states, 'STANDBY' otherwise.

indicators.label: OPERATE button
indicators.meaning: PGXL state: IDLE/OPERATE/TRANSMIT_* show OPERATE; STANDBY/POWERUP/FAULT show STANDBY.

related_user_tasks: Put the PGXL amplifier in OPERATE
Put the PGXL amplifier in STANDBY
Monitor forward power and SWR at the amplifier output
Watch PGXL temperature, drain current, and mains voltage

entries.purpose: Controls a 4O3A Antenna Genius switch: discovers devices, lets you connect manually by IP, and selects antennas per radio port with band-aware TX/RX permission colouring and AUTO mode.
entries.how_to_open: Hidden until an Antenna Genius (LDG-AT) is detected or manually connected. When present, toggle with the 'AG' tray button on the right sidebar.

controls.label: Device combo
controls.behavior: Selects which discovered AG device to connect to.
controls.notes: Auto-selects and connects when the first device is discovered.

controls.label: Connect / Disconnect
controls.behavior: Connects to the selected device (or the Manual IP if none selected); becomes 'Disconnect' when connected.

controls.label: Manual IP
controls.behavior: Enter an IP and press Enter to connect to port 9007.
controls.notes: Invalid addresses produce a red 'Invalid IP address' status.

controls.label: Port A antenna buttons
controls.behavior: Click to select an antenna on Port A; click again to deselect (antenna=0).
controls.notes: Disabled/dim if the antenna is already selected on Port B. Blue = TX+RX, amber = RX only, dim = no permission on current band.

controls.label: Port A AUTO
controls.behavior: Enables band-follow on Port A; calls setAutoMode(1, on).

controls.label: Port B antenna buttons
controls.valid_range: same antenna list as Port A
controls.behavior: Click to select an antenna on Port B; click again to deselect.
controls.notes: Port B section hidden if the AG device reports only one radio port.

controls.label: Port B AUTO
controls.behavior: Enables band-follow on Port B; calls setAutoMode(2, on).

indicators.label: Status label
indicators.meaning: Discovery/connection state of the Antenna Genius.

indicators.label: Port A band
indicators.meaning: Active band on Port A (AG-reported or frequency-derived).

indicators.label: Port A antenna
indicators.meaning: Selected antenna; red when TXing, orange when TX routed to alt antenna or inhibit is asserted.

indicators.label: Port B band
indicators.meaning: Active band on Port B.

indicators.label: Port B antenna
indicators.meaning: Selected antenna for Port B.

related_user_tasks: Auto-discover an Antenna Genius on the LAN
Manually connect to an AG over a remote network
Select an antenna for Port A or Port B
Enable AUTO mode so the AG follows radio band changes
Spot which antennas cannot TX on the current band (amber or dim)
Swap radios that share the AG (antennas in use by the other port are locked out)

entries.purpose: Runs an Expert-style TCI WebSocket server so third-party logging, digital-mode, and SDR software (Log4OM, SunSDR tools, etc.) can read and control the radio over the TCI protocol.
entries.how_to_open: Hidden by default. Toggle with the 'TCI' tray button on the right sidebar.

controls.label: RX1..RX4 gain+meter
controls.behavior: Combined meter/slider; drag sets the TCI RX gain for the channel and emits tciRxGainChanged.
controls.notes: One setting_key per channel (TciRxGain1, TciRxGain2, TciRxGain3, TciRxGain4).

controls.label: TX gain+meter
controls.behavior: Drags set the TCI TX gain and emit tciTxGainChanged.
controls.notes: TciServer::setTxGain persists TciTxGain internally; UI mirrors the stored value.

controls.label: Port
controls.behavior: Changing the port restarts the server if enabled.
controls.notes: Out-of-range values snap to 50001.

controls.label: Enable
controls.behavior: Starts or stops the TCI server; emits tciToggled.
controls.notes: If bind fails the toggle snaps back to off and status shows '(port in use)'.

controls.label: RX/TX slice-assignment labels
controls.behavior: Show which slice currently drives each RX/TX row (shares DAX channel mapping).

indicators.label: Server status
indicators.meaning: Server state and connected client count; colour changes to red on bind failure.

related_user_tasks: Enable the TCI server for Log4OM / SunSDR clients
Change the TCI port
Adjust TCI RX gain per channel
Adjust TCI TX gain
Autostart TCI on launch
See how many TCI clients are connected

entries.purpose: Integrates AetherSDR with a station MQTT broker: subscribe to topics, log incoming messages, overlay '*'-prefixed topics on the panadapter, and publish canned messages with user-editable buttons.
entries.how_to_open: Hidden by default. Toggle with the MQTT tray button on the right sidebar.

controls.label: Host
controls.behavior: Broker hostname or IP.
controls.notes: Persisted when the Enable button is clicked on.

controls.label: Port
controls.behavior: Broker TCP port.
controls.notes: Auto-switches between 1883 and 8883 when TLS is toggled.

controls.label: User
controls.behavior: Broker username (optional).

controls.label: Pass
controls.behavior: Broker password (optional).
controls.notes: Masked (password echo).

controls.label: Topics
controls.behavior: Comma-separated topic list; prefix with '*' to also overlay on the panadapter.
controls.notes: Example: '*rotator/pos, *ant/selected, station/log'.

controls.label: TLS
controls.behavior: Enable TLS encryption; auto-flips port 1883 ↔ 8883.
controls.notes: Shows/hides the CA cert row.

controls.label: CA cert
controls.behavior: Path to a CA certificate file; blank means the system CA bundle.
controls.notes: Row visible only when TLS is checked.

controls.label: Enable (Off/On)
controls.behavior: Connects or disconnects; emits connectRequested / disconnectRequested and saves all settings.

controls.label: Publish buttons
controls.behavior: Click publishes the configured payload to the configured topic via MqttClient::publish.
controls.notes: Only active while connected; stored as JSON under MqttButtons.

controls.label: Edit / Done
controls.behavior: Enters button-edit mode: clicking a button opens an Edit dialog, right-click removes, and a '+' tile adds up to 12 buttons.

controls.label: Message log
controls.behavior: Displays received messages as 'topic: value' lines.

indicators.label: Status label
indicators.meaning: Connection state with colour: green when connected, grey when disconnected, default on error.

related_user_tasks: Connect to a station MQTT broker
Subscribe to rotator / antenna switch topics
Overlay an MQTT value on the panadapter (prefix topic with *)
Publish a canned message with a button (e.g. rotator preset)
Add or remove custom publish buttons
Enable TLS with a custom CA certificate

entries.purpose: Container for a single panadapter display (FFT spectrum + waterfall) with a title bar (drag grip, pop-out, maximize, close) and an optional CW decode panel underneath for off-air Morse decoding.
entries.how_to_open: Always visible in the main window (centre). Additional panadapters can be added in multi-slice mode; each gets its own title bar with pop-out, maximize, and close buttons.

controls.label: Slice title
controls.behavior: Shows which slice is bound to this panadapter.

controls.label: ⬈ / ↩ (pop-out/dock)
controls.behavior: Pops the panadapter out into a floating window or docks it back; emits popOutClicked or dockClicked.
controls.notes: Hidden in single-pan mode.

controls.label: □ (maximize)
controls.behavior: Maximizes this panadapter in a multi-pan layout; emits maximizeRequested.
controls.notes: Hidden in single-pan mode.

controls.label: × (close)
controls.behavior: Closes this panadapter; emits closeRequested.
controls.notes: Hidden in single-pan mode.

controls.label: Spectrum / waterfall
controls.behavior: Click activates the panadapter; drag to tune, scroll to zoom (SpectrumWidget).

controls.label: CW stats label
controls.behavior: Shows detected CW pitch and speed.

controls.label: Sens
controls.behavior: Filters low-confidence decodes; higher = stricter.
controls.notes: Maps 0-100 to cost threshold 1.0-0.1.

controls.label: 🔒P (Lock Pitch)
controls.behavior: Locks the CW decoder pitch to the current tuned frequency.

controls.label: 🔒S (Lock Speed)
controls.behavior: Locks the CW decoder speed to the current WPM.

controls.label: Lo (pitch min)
controls.behavior: Minimum pitch the CW decoder searches; clamped ≤ Hi.

controls.label: Hi (pitch max)
controls.behavior: Maximum pitch the CW decoder searches; clamped ≥ Lo.

controls.label: CPY ALL
controls.behavior: Copies the full decoded text to the clipboard.

controls.label: CPY VIS
controls.behavior: Copies only the text currently visible in the scroll area.

controls.label: CLR
controls.behavior: Clears the CW decode buffer.

controls.label: ✕ (close CW)
controls.behavior: Hides the CW decode panel; emits cwPanelCloseRequested.

controls.label: CW decode text
controls.behavior: Read-only rolling display of decoded CW coloured by confidence.
controls.notes: Colours: <0.15 green, <0.35 yellow, <0.60 orange, >=0.60 red.

indicators.label: CW stats
indicators.meaning: Detected pitch and speed from the ggmorse decoder.

indicators.label: CW hint
indicators.meaning: Reminder that the CW decoder needs PC audio routing to work.

related_user_tasks: Click the spectrum to activate a panadapter (multi-slice mode)
Pop a panadapter out into its own window
Maximize one panadapter to fill the main area
Close an extra panadapter
Turn on the CW decoder to read Morse off-air
Tune CW decoder sensitivity to reject noise
Lock CW decoder pitch or speed once tracking is good
Copy decoded CW text to the clipboard
